page: Microsoft Azure D2nlds_v6 is a member of the Dnldv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This virtual machine features 2 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture with an Intel processor. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

The instance includes 118 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable. It is classified under the network-optimized and low-memory tiers of the Dnldv6 family. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

The primary tradeoff of the D2nlds_v6 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:2, which limits its utility for memory-intensive applications. However, the dedicated CPU allocation and local disk space make it suitable for network-optimized workloads, lightweight web serving, basic routing, or small-scale database tasks that require dedicated compute resources but have minimal memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure D2nlds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine within the Dnldv6 server family, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory, establishing a low-memory profile of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with 118 GB of local storage and supports premium storage configurations. Designed with a network-optimized architecture, this server lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The low memory-to-vCPU ratio represents a clear tradeoff, making the instance less suitable for memory-heavy applications. Instead, the dedicated CPU allocation and local disk capacity position it for network-focused workloads, lightweight proxy services, and small-scale utility tasks requiring consistent compute performance without high memory demands.
